tex:
(a) Write the number six and a half million in figures.

(b) Write 6538 correct to the nearest ten.

(c) Work out $6 \times 5 + 12 \div 3$.

(d) 9 \quad 16 \quad 18 \quad 29 \quad 57 \quad 64 \quad 87 \quad 96
From this list of numbers, write down

(i)  a factor of 48,

 a cube number,

 a prime number.

(e) Find the value of $\sqrt{0.001225}$.

(f) Find the reciprocal of 8.

(g) Find the value of $8^0$.

(h) (i)  Write 180 as a product of its prime factors.

 Find the lowest common multiple (LCM) of 160 and 180.

(i) The mass of an aircraft, $m$ tonnes, is 473 tonnes, correct to the nearest tonne.

Complete this statement about the value of $m$.

(a) Write down the number of sides of a hexagon.

(b) 
In triangle $ABC$, $AB = AC$.

(i)  Write down the mathematical name for this type of triangle.

 Measure angle $CAB$.

 Write down the mathematical name for angle $CAB$.

(c) Show that the interior angle of a regular pentagon is $108°$.

(d) 
$ABCD$ is a parallelogram.\\
The reflex angle at $D$ is $248°$.

Find angle $DCB$.

(e) The angles of a triangle are in the ratio $3 : 5 : 7$.

Find the size of the largest angle in this triangle.

Sachin, his wife and three children go on a coach holiday.

(a) Each adult ticket costs \$375 and each child ticket costs \$194.

Work out the total cost of the tickets.

(b) A meal costs \$110 plus a service charge of 18\%.

Calculate the total cost of the meal.

(c) One day, the temperature at midday is $16°$C.\\
At midnight the temperature has fallen by $23°$C.

Work out the temperature at midnight.

(d) Sachin spends \$768 on holiday.\\
He spends $\dfrac{3}{8}$ of this amount on presents.

Find how much he spends on presents.

(e) There are 604 passengers on the holiday.

(i)  The coach company uses coaches which can carry 46 passengers.

Work out the number of coaches needed.

 268 of the 604 passengers are women.

Find the percentage of the passengers that are women.

(f) A coach travels at an average speed of 54\,km/h.

Find how long, in hours and minutes, this coach takes to travel 126\,km.

(a) 
The diagram shows a right-angled triangular prism.

(i)  On the 1\,cm$^2$ grid, complete a net of this prism.\\
One face has been drawn for you.

[Grid with one 7cm × 3cm rectangle drawn]

 Work out the volume of this prism.

(b) 
The diagram shows a rectangle with 6 congruent circles inside.\\
Each circle touches the adjacent circles and the sides of the rectangle.\\
The radius of each circle is 8\,cm.

(i)  Show that the length of the rectangle is 48\,cm.

 Find the area of the rectangle.\\
Give the units of your answer.

 Calculate the percentage of the rectangle that is shaded.

(a) The grid shows a point $A$.

(i)  Write down the coordinates of point $A$.

 On the grid, plot the point $B$ at $(-1, 3)$.

 $C$ is a point on the grid whose coordinates are whole numbers.

On the grid, mark a point $C$ so that triangle $ABC$ is isosceles.

(b) 
The diagram shows a rhombus.

(i)  Write down the order of rotational symmetry.

 On the diagram, draw all the lines of symmetry.

(c) The grid shows triangles $A$, $B$ and $C$.

(i)  Describe fully the **single** transformation that maps triangle $A$ onto triangle $B$.

 Describe fully the **single** transformation that maps triangle $A$ onto triangle $C$.

 Draw the image of

\begin{parts}

(d) triangle $A$ after a translation by the vector $\begin{pmatrix} -5 \\ 3 \end{pmatrix}$,

(e) triangle $A$ after a reflection in the line $y = -2$.

\end{parts}

(a) A football team has $w$ wins and $d$ draws.\\
The team scores 3 points for each win and 1 point for each draw.

Write an expression, in terms of $w$ and $d$, for the total number of points scored by the team.

(b) Athletic, Rovers and United are three football teams.

Athletic have a point score of $x$.\\
Rovers have 12 points more than Athletic's point score.\\
United have 3 points fewer than twice Athletic's point score.

The total point score of all three teams is 121.

Use this information to write down an equation in terms of $x$.\\
Solve your equation to work out the point score for each team.

(c) Simplify.

(i)  $4a - 3b + 5a + 6b$

 $6(2x + 1) - 5(x - 2)$

(d) Solve the simultaneous equations.\\
You must show all your working.
\begin{align*}
3x + 5y &= 11\\
2x - 3y &= 20
\end{align*}

(a) A class of 15 students take two tests in science, paper 1 and paper 2.\\
The scores for each student are shown in the table.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Paper 1 & 5 & 11 & 12 & 20 & 26 & 31 & 32 & 37 & 45 & 47 & 51 & 54 & 55 & 23 & 42 \\
\hline
Paper 2 & 12 & 15 & 27 & 27 & 51 & 44 & 52 & 54 & 54 & 74 & 66 & 72 & 78 & 30 & 58 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
[Scatter diagram with axes: Paper 1 (0-60) horizontal, Paper 2 (0-80) vertical]
(i)  Complete the scatter diagram.\\
The first thirteen points have been plotted for you.

 What type of correlation is shown in the scatter diagram?

 On the grid, draw a line of best fit.

 Another student scores 24 on paper 1.

Use your line of best fit to find an estimate for their score on paper 2.

(b) 140 students choose which subjects they want to study.
\begin{parts}

(c) 122 students choose biology ($B$).

(d) 55 students choose chemistry ($C$).

(e) 2 students do not choose biology and do not choose chemistry.

(i)  Complete the Venn diagram.

 One of these students is picked at random.

Find the probability that this student chooses biology and chemistry.

\end{parts}

(d) 55 students choose chemistry ($C$). (e) 2 students do not choose biology and do not choose chemistry. (i) Complete the Venn diagram. One of these students is picked at random. Find the probability that this student chooses biology and chemistry. \end{parts}

tex:
The grid shows a line $L$.

(a) Find the equation of line $L$.\\
Give your answer in the form $y = mx + c$.

(b) (i)  Complete the table of values for $y = 2x + 5$.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
$x$ & $-5$ & $-3$ & $0$ \\
\hline
$y$ & $-5$ & & $5$ \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

 On the grid, draw the graph of $y = 2x + 5$.

(c) Write down the coordinates of the point which lies on both line $L$ and the graph of $y = 2x + 5$.

(d) Write down the equation of the line that is parallel to $y = 2x + 5$ and passes through the point $(0, 18)$.
texHtml:
The grid shows a line $L$. (a) Find the equation of line $L$.\\ Give your answer in the form $y = mx + c$. (b) (i) Complete the table of values for $y = 2x + 5$.
$x$$-5$$-3$$0$
$y$$-5$$5$
On the grid, draw the graph of $y = 2x + 5$. (c) Write down the coordinates of the point which lies on both line $L$ and the graph of $y = 2x + 5$. (d) Write down the equation of the line that is parallel to $y = 2x + 5$ and passes through the point $(0, 18)$.

tex:
(a) Complete the table of values for $y = \dfrac{12}{x}$, $x \neq 0$.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
$x$ & $-6$ & $-4$ & $-3$ & $-2$ & $-1$ & $1$ & $2$ & $3$ & $4$ & $6$ \\
\hline
$y$ & & $-3$ & & $-6$ & & & $6$ & & $3$ & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(b) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = \dfrac{12}{x}$ for $-6 \leqslant x \leqslant -1$ and $1 \leqslant x \leqslant 6$.

(c) On the grid, draw the line $y = 5$.

(d) Use your graph to solve the equation $\dfrac{12}{x} = 5$.
texHtml:
(a) Complete the table of values for $y = \dfrac{12}{x}$, $x \neq 0$.
$x$$-6$$-4$$-3$$-2$$-1$$1$$2$$3$$4$$6$
$y$$-3$$-6$$6$$3$
(b) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = \dfrac{12}{x}$ for $-6 \leqslant x \leqslant -1$ and $1 \leqslant x \leqslant 6$. (c) On the grid, draw the line $y = 5$. (d) Use your graph to solve the equation $\dfrac{12}{x} = 5$.
